name: Cincinnati Chili
recipeInstructions:
1. Add ground beef to water in a 4-quart pot and stir until the beef separates into a very fine texture.
2. Bring the mixture to a boil slowly, then reduce heat to maintain a simmer for about half an hour.
3. Add all other ingredients to the pot.
4. Stir to blend everything thoroughly, then bring back to a boil.
5. Reduce heat and let the chili simmer uncovered for about three hours until it reaches the desired consistency.
6. If using beans or additional vegetables (e.g., kidney beans, chopped onions), add them during the last hour of cooking.
7. Remove the pot from heat and allow it to cool slightly before serving. The chili should be refrigerated overnight so that fat can rise to the top; skim off any excess fat before reheating and serving.
8. Serve "three-way" with macaroni or spaghetti and grated cheese, "four-way" with beans added, or "five-way" with both beans and chopped onions.
This recipe serves eight people.
